summary: encrata-cli postinstall script executes system reconnaissance and outbound HTTPS
details: The package declares a postinstall hook (`"postinstall": "node install.js"`) that runs install.js automatically on `npm install`. install.js loads `child_process` and `https`, branches on `process.platform` to spawn OS-specific shell commands (process listing / environment enumeration), and issues an outbound `https.get(...)` call. This shape — lifecycle-time platform-gated system reconnaissance combined with outbound network I/O — is the canonical install-time information-stealer pattern and has no legitimate purpose in a CLI installer script. Installing the package causes the installer's host metadata and/or environment data to be sent to a network destination embedded in install.js without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
